evidence_sentence: Tre6P synthesis by TPS1 is essential for embryogenesis and postembryonic growth

mechanism: WRKY TF promotes integument cell elongation and tannin synthesis
evidence_sentence: TTG2 gene acts maternally to promote cell expansion in integuments

mechanism: Warm temperature disrupts Evening Complex (ELF3/ELF4/LUX) assembly via ELF3 prion-like domain phase transition.
evidence_sentence: Elevated temperature inactivates ELF3 and releases thermoresponsive growth

mechanism: Warm temperature activates HSFA1d heat-shock factor, which stabilizes PIF4 (thermomorphogenesis intermediate â€” JUDGE S003 iter 2).
evidence_sentence: Warm temperature markedly enhanced daytime accumulation of HSFA1d in light-dependent manner

mechanism: Warm temperature destabilizes HY5 protein.
evidence_sentence: High temperature triggers the degradation of HY5

mechanism: High ambient temperature increases PIF4 protein abundance and activity, independent of light
evidence_sentence: PIF4 is required for thermomorphogenic elongation of hypocotyls and petioles at elevated ambient temperature

mechanism: UBP15 deubiquitinates growth-promoting substrates; promotes cell proliferation
evidence_sentence: UBP15 influences seed size by promoting cell proliferation in the maternal integuments

mechanism: Monomeric UVR8 binds COP1 and prevents COP1-mediated HY5 ubiquitination.

mechanism: UVR8 positively regulates HY5 expression through COP1 sequestration and direct HY5 gene activation.
evidence_sentence: UVR8 associates constitutively with chromatin regions of several UV-B-activated genes, including the HY5 genomic locus

mechanism: UV-B destabilizes PIF5.
evidence_sentence: PIF4-HA and PIF5-HA protein levels decrease under UV-B in UVR8-dependent manner

mechanism: VAL1 silences ABI3 via chromatin

mechanism: VAL1 silences FUS3 via chromatin repression
evidence_sentence: FUS3 shows elevated expression in val1 val2 seedlings

mechanism: VAL1 silences LEC2 in seedlings

mechanism: VAL1 physically interacts with PKL and recruits it to LAFL loci
evidence_sentence: VAL1 and VAL2 physically interact with PKL in vivo and promote efficient PKL recruitment at thousands of target genes

mechanism: VAL2 silences ABI3 redundantly
evidence_sentence: ABI3 upregulated in val1 val2 seedlings

mechanism: VAL2 silences FUS3 redundantly
evidence_sentence: FUS3 elevated in val1 val2 seedlings

mechanism: VAL2 B3 repressor silences LEC1 redundantly with VAL1
evidence_sentence: val1 val2 double mutants show strong upregulation of LEC1

mechanism: VAL2 silences LEC2 redundantly
evidence_sentence: LEC2 strongly up-regulated in val1 val2

mechanism: VAL2 recruits PKL redundantly
evidence_sentence: VAL2 interacts with PKL and recruits it to target genes

mechanism: VIN3 PHD protein binds H3K9me2 and recruits PRC2 for H3K27me3 silencing
evidence_sentence: all VIN3 family proteins preferentially bind to H3K9me2 peptides in vitro through PHD motifs

mechanism: Vernalization (extended cold) upregulates AGL24 expression
evidence_sentence: Vernalization increases AGL24 expression

mechanism: Vernalizing cold progressively activates VIN3 via H3K4me3
evidence_sentence: VIN3 mRNA is present at very low abundance during growth at warm temperatures, with expression increasing progressively during vernalization

mechanism: WOX11/12 upregulate LBD16 to drive root founder cell fate transition [Edge represents composite family; evidence drawn from curated E221 (WOX11->LBD16).]
evidence_sentence: WOX11 and WOX12 then act redundantly to upregulate LBD16 and LBD29

mechanism: Warm ambient temperature triggers FT upregulation via multiple routes (H2A.Z eviction, PIF4 activation, SVP degradation, FLM splicing)
evidence_sentence: A mild increase in temperature can trigger floral transition in Arabidopsis...mainly attributed to an increase in expression of the major flowering-promoting genes FLOWERING LOCUS T (FT)

mechanism: Warm temperature releases ELF3/HY5 repression and stabilizes PIF4 via reduced phyB activity
evidence_sentence: Warm temperatures reduce the binding of the evening complex to chromatin and eventually promotes the expression of PIF4

mechanism: Warm ambient temperature triggers rapid SVP protein degradation via the 26S proteasome pathway
evidence_sentence: SVP protein is rapidly degraded by the 26S proteasome pathway in response to warm ambient temperatures

mechanism: flavin monooxygenase converts IPA to IAA in endosperm post-fertilization
evidence_sentence: Auxin biosynthesis following fertilization is dependent on paternally-expressed YUC10 and TAR1

mechanism: flavin monooxygenase auxin biosynthesis
evidence_sentence: Two YUC genes expressed in endosperm, YUC10 and YUC11

mechanism: YUC4 flavin monooxygenase converts IPA to IAA
evidence_sentence: First step in LR initiation involves formation of auxin maximum dependent on influx carrier AUX1 and activation of YUCCA4-mediated auxin biosynthesis

mechanism: YUC8 catalyzes IPyA->IAA, the final step of auxin biosynthesis.
evidence_sentence: YUC8 expression well correlated with PIF7 activity

mechanism: YUC8 (flavin monooxygenase) converts indole-3-pyruvate to IAA (auxin) in the YUC-dependent tryptophan pathway
evidence_sentence: YUCCA enzymes convert indole-pyruvic acid to indole-3-acetic acid (IAA, auxin), the rate-limiting step in tryptophan-derived auxin biosynthesis

mechanism: ZTL directly interacts with CO and destabilizes it, opposing FKF1
evidence_sentence: ZTL destabilizes CO protein by directly interacting with the protein in Arabidopsis; FKF1 and ZTL seem to have antagonistic roles in regulating CO protein stability

mechanism: Cold-induced COOLAIR antisense lncRNA accelerates FLC transcriptional repression and reduces H3K36me3
evidence_sentence: Cold-induced antisense transcripts (COOLAIR) are required for coordinated switching of opposing chromatin states at FLC

mechanism: miR156 targets SPL9 transcript by cleavage/translational repression
evidence_sentence: miR156 targets 10 members of the SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN LIKE (SPL) family of transcription factors (SPL2, SPL3, SPL4, SPL5, SPL6, SPL9, SPL10, SPL11, SPL13, SPL15).

mechanism: miR156 represses SPLs which activate miR172; sequential action
evidence_sentence: sequential action of miR156 and miR172 regulates developmental timing

mechanism: miR172 targets AP2 transcript
evidence_sentence: In Arabidopsis miR172 negatively regulate the expression of AP2 and five AP2-like genes including TARGET OF EAT 1 (TOE1), TOE2, TOE3, SCHNARCHZAPFEN (SNZ) and SCHLAFMUTZE (SMZ).

mechanism: miR172 downregulates AP2 by translational repression (predominantly) rather than RNA cleavage
evidence_sentence: miR172 downregulates these target genes by a translational mechanism rather than by RNA cleavage

mechanism: microRNA-mediated cleavage of GRF1-4, GRF7-9 mRNAs
evidence_sentence: miR396 targets and induces cleavage of AtGRF mRNA (AtGRF1-4, AtGRF7-9)

mechanism: Cytokinin receptor histidine kinase composite (AHK2/AHK3/AHK4) activates B-type ARR transcription factors via AHP phosphorelay.
evidence_sentence: ahk2 ahk3 double mutants exhibited a semidwarf phenotype as to shoots, such as a reduced leaf size and a reduced inflorescence stem length, and AHK receptors activate the AHP-ARR phosphorelay downstream.

mechanism: EIN3/EIL1 recruit the FLD histone demethylase to the FLC locus, reducing H3K4me2 and repressing FLC transcription.
evidence_sentence: The ein3 eil1 double mutant had higher levels of H3K4me2 at the FLC locus and exhibited a late flowering phenotype, suggesting EIN3/EIL1 recruit FLD demethylase to repress FLC.

mechanism: Auxin-induced expression of LBD16/LBD29 drives lateral root founder cell specification and primordium initiation downstream of ARF7/ARF19.
evidence_sentence: Auxin induces the expression of LBD16 and LBD29 through ARF7 and ARF19; lbd16 lbd29 double mutants show drastically reduced lateral root density.

mechanism: Auxin is the master hormonal promoter of lateral root initiation, founder-cell specification, and primordium emergence.
evidence_sentence: Local auxin biosynthesis via TAA1/TAR and YUCCA enzymes, followed by polar transport through PINs, establishes the auxin maximum required for lateral root initiation and density.

mechanism: BZR1 and BES1 (BZR2) are close paralogs with redundant function. They heterodimerize and bind many shared genomic targets; they behave as a composite BZR/BES transcriptional activator.
evidence_sentence: BZR1 and BES1/BZR2 are close paralogs acting redundantly; gain-of-function bzr1-1D phenocopies bes1-D, with constitutive BR responses including enhanced growth and elongation.

mechanism: BIN2 phosphorylates and inactivates BZR1/BES1, blocking BR-responsive cell-elongation transcription. bin2-1 gain-of-function causes dwarfism.
evidence_sentence: At maturity, the main inflorescence stem was about one-half of the wild-type height in bin2 gain-of-function mutants; BIN2 is a negative regulator of brassinosteroid signaling and plant height.

mechanism: Active PHYB (high R:FR) represses BRC1 transcription in axillary buds; phyB loss-of-function or low R:FR derepresses BRC1 and enforces bud dormancy.
evidence_sentence: Active phyB down-regulates BRC1 in axillary buds under high R:FR; phyB mutants have elevated BRC1, increased bud dormancy, and fewer branches, phenocopying shade avoidance.
